title: Berbere Stew
description: A tangy and comforting Ethiopian stew featuring dried mint, served in a corn-husk wrap. This stew is a flavorful and healthy option, perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the garlic and cook for another minute. Add the carrots and zucchini and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the bell pepper and cook for another 5 minutes. Add the Berbere spice blend and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly. Add the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly. Add the vegetable broth and water, and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes. Stir in the dried mint and season with salt and black pepper. Serve hot in corn-husk wraps.
Cook the corn-husk wraps according to the package instructions.
Prepare all the ingredients according to the package instructions or as needed.
Heat the oil in a small pan over low heat.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the carrots and zucchini and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the bell pepper and cook for another 5 minutes.
Add the Berbere spice blend and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the vegetable broth and water, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
Stir in the dried mint and season with salt and black pepper.
